118739.explanation: SSM wants to better hear and involve residents (more participation, service-oriented government), but a referendum is not included as an instrument in the program.

118744.explanation: SSM does not want ‘block boxes’/distribution centers that take up a lot of space and contribute little to local employment.

118746.explanation: SSM wants to transform 'Poland hotels' in village centers into senior housing and better align housing with livability. Housing on one's own business premises fits within this solution.

118750.explanation: SSM wants safe streets and less nuisance (including tackling firework bombs). A fireworks show can be a fun and safe alternative to the national fireworks ban. SSM prefers the municipality organizes this together with residents.

118751.explanation: Bulky waste can be delivered for free at the recycling center in Zaltbommel. Collecting waste costs money and must be paid for somehow. If bulky waste is free, the tax for waste rises for everyone. SSM chooses that the user pays.

118753.explanation: Accessibility is important, and SSM wants to encourage entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible. SSM thinks encouragement and loosening rules for entrepreneurs are first steps, rather than immediately offering financial support.

118754.explanation: SSM wants to accelerate housing development through planned building in/on village centers and ensuring 'the right type in the right place.' Scattered housing on agricultural land does not fit this direction. Through the VAB policy, farmers can already build homes.

118756.explanation: SSM emphasizes community and engagement; it ensures that residents are well informed.

118758.explanation: SSM wants every child in Maasdriel to be introduced to sports and culture. Clubs receive subsidies for this. This can be music lessons but also sports.

118764.explanation: If there is a need for a women’s shelter, then it is necessary. For example, if this care is too far away or waiting lists are too long. SSM focuses on care close by and better support.

118769.explanation: SSM wants to expand all village centers and accelerate housing development. New construction only within existing boundaries does not fit this approach.

118770.explanation: SSM finds it important to help those on social assistance so they have the chance to rebuild their lives. Delivering a social return or doing work with sufficient income to leave social assistance stands in the way.

118772.explanation: Broadening poverty/income limits helps to reach more vulnerable people. SSM wants social and fair policies with less administrative hassle.

118775.explanation: 30 km/h speed limit in built-up areas is established policy (GVVP) and is being implemented. SSM sticks to this policy. It helps with better traffic design and safer routes, especially in combination with bike paths.

118776.explanation: Choice based on religion/culture fits within the Together Strong philosophy. SSM especially wants faster, more accessible support and care nearby. Choosing other care may mean that care is available outside the municipality of Maasdriel.

118777.explanation: SSM wants to organize care better and faster; choices in youth care should be based on quality and family needs, not primarily on price.

118778.explanation: SSM wants to keep local taxes as low as possible and not let property tax (OZB) rise without reason; working efficiently and having realistic plans belong to this.

118779.explanation: SSM wants to abolish the dog tax.

118782.explanation: SSM focuses on local entrepreneurs and livability. Sunday shopping contributes to this.

118783.explanation: SSM follows national legislation here to maintain a good relationship with the supervising province. To ensure sufficient housing is available for local needs, SSM focuses on more affordable homes and speeding up housing construction.

118785.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land are encouraged in designated areas in the Vision Sun and Wind. SSM sticks to this course. However, SSM finds it important to first focus on solar panels on building roofs before using meadows.

118789.explanation: SSM wants more affordable housing and to build for young people and seniors. We adhere to the chosen percentage of 35% social rental and 35% affordable (up to 420,000 euros).

118790.explanation: SSM also wants to build higher (e.g., adding stories) and more apartments, especially for seniors and starters, fitting with the village.

118791.explanation: SSM maintains the policy against subdivision/multiple room rentals to prevent migrant workers from settling in the village centers.

118792.explanation: SSM finds accessibility, traffic safety, and better traffic flow important.

118793.explanation: Sand trucks cause nuisance in Kerkdriel. Many residents wish to move these companies. Costs must be proportional to the desired effect.

118794.explanation: SSM wants De Kreek back and future-proof, especially for swimming lessons and sports. De Kreek may grow into 'the central meeting point,' unless other locations come forward for this.

118795.explanation: There is a challenge for sustainable energy. SSM acknowledges the contribution of existing wind turbines but there are also other options like solar panels on roofs. SSM wants to prioritize these options first.

118796.explanation: SSM wants every sport to run smoothly and the municipality to fairly facilitate with subsidies/accommodations; co-payment can fit within that policy.

118797.explanation: SSM wants better traffic flow and extra roads toward the A2 and to direct freight traffic outside village centers, but a specific ring road near Kerkdriel like the ZOR does not need to be included in the planning.

118739.explanation: Only on topics that matter and can affect citizens financially or socially.

118746.explanation: There must also be opportunities for clustered housing.

118750.explanation: Stimulate initiatives from the population. Not always wanting to take the lead yourself.

118751.explanation: A contribution for exceptional deposits must be paid by the provider.

118753.explanation: The entrepreneur excludes themselves if no provisions are made.

118754.explanation: It should be possible within existing regulations. VAB.

118756.explanation: Value for money. Utility and necessity may be converted into subsidies.

118758.explanation: Within the curriculum, space could be made for music education.

118764.explanation: If there is a need and regular care is lacking.

118769.explanation: Infill development always has priority, but possibilities also exist outside the village boundaries.

118770.explanation: There are people who are unable to comply with this.

118775.explanation: Depending on traffic conditions and safety.

118777.explanation: Provided this does not come at the expense of quality.

118778.explanation: We are at a good average.

118783.explanation: Participating in society also simply means waiting in line.

118785.explanation: First fill the roofs. See vision on solar and wind.

118793.explanation: In this case, a 'dying house' construction may suffice.

118794.explanation: There are better initiatives on the inside of the dikes.

118795.explanation: We must meet the wind vision and execute our RES submission. Support from the population is leading here.

118796.explanation: A nice investment for amateur sports.

118797.explanation: Solutions on the existing infrastructure network must be investigated first.

118739.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel stands for early involvement and influence of citizens in policy. Referenda oversimplify complex issues too much. The municipal council should make decisions.

118744.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel opposes distribution centers, among other things because of extra traffic pressure and the limited contribution to the local economy.

118746.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants more possibilities for housing on entrepreneurs' own premises. However, restrictions can be imposed if other interests weigh heavier.

118750.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel has no specific position on fireworks shows.

118751.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel stands for cost-covering fees and prudent use of funds. Bulk waste should be collected free of charge up to a certain maximum.

118753.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel considers accessibility of buildings important for everyone. The municipality could provide support under conditions.

118754.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants creative housing solutions and to give entrepreneurs space. However, restrictions can be imposed if other interests weigh heavier.

118756.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel has no specific position on local media.

118758.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel focuses on English, technology, and ICT. Music lessons are not a priority.

118764.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel has no specific position on a women's refuge in the municipality. However, it is essential that everyone can feel safe in the municipality.

118769.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to preserve the identity of the villages but also increase the building pace for housing. However, restrictions can be imposed if other interests weigh heavier.

118770.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants benefit recipients (social assistance) to provide a counter-performance where meaningful.

118772.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to offer help to vulnerable people and adjust poverty policy to inflation.

118775.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to sensibly invest in increasing traffic safety; 30 km/h where necessary and meaningful, 50 km/h on through roads.

118776.explanation: Freedom of choice in general is good. This specific form of freedom of choice goes too far because it can lead to discrimination against healthcare personnel and conflicts with the separation of church and state.

118777.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to focus on quality and results, not only on price.

118778.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to keep housing costs as low as possible, but the municipal budget must be balanced. The starting point is no increase (beyond inflation).

118779.explanation: Dog tax is an outdated tax that causes extra burdens for both residents and the municipality.

118782.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to support entrepreneurs; Sunday shopping offers opportunities.

118783.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants priority for local residents in social housing. In line with current cabinet policy, VVD-Maasdriel wants the priority for status holders to be banned and the target for housing status holders to be abolished.

118785.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel supports small-scale energy generation. This could take place on agricultural land for own use (unless other locations are better and/or other interests oppose it).

118789.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to address the problems faced by first-time buyers and achieve a good mix of housing. Significantly more social rental housing must be built, but VVD-Maasdriel opposes central quotas. More building freedom and fewer rules are desired.

118790.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to preserve the identity of the towns; high residential towers do not fit with that.

118791.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants to maintain livability; subdividing houses into multiple rooms does not align with that.

118792.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants good accessibility with sufficient parking spaces.

118793.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel supports relocating sand companies to alternative locations, but not at any (financial) cost or with unacceptable consequences for other villages.

118794.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel acknowledges the importance of a central meeting place for the residents of Kerkdriel. There may be other alternatives that are better than De Kreek as the central meeting point.

118795.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel opposes new large wind turbines that disrupt the landscape. The emphasis in the Netherlands should be on offshore wind energy, not wind turbines in central Netherlands.

118796.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel believes it is important to first establish a broad sports and facilities policy in the municipality, including rules for subsidies. A subsidy for a new track for the Bicycle Cross Club Ammersoyen should be assessed against this.

118797.explanation: VVD-Maasdriel wants better accessibility of the villages and targeted investment in traffic safety. It is important that there is sufficient financial coverage for this.

118739.explanation: In our municipality, alongside the possibility of referenda, we want to gain experience with a form of Citizens' Assembly. The gap between policy and daily practice must become smaller. We want many more people to reengage rather than disengage from our democratic governance.

118744.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the economy in Bommelerwaard currently focuses too much on logistics and greenhouse horticulture. These are sectors that use a lot of space, consume a lot of energy, and generate few well-paid jobs.

118746.explanation: Housing must take place on the company's own grounds. Good regulations and enforcement in the housing of labor migrants remain important to prevent abuses.

118750.explanation: The best New Year's party is one celebrated together, with fireworks shows in the towns organized in cooperation with the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year’s Eve together.

118751.explanation: Bulky waste, old mattresses, and other rubbish on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. Besides the option to bring these items to the recycling center, it is good when it is possible to have bulky waste collected for free.

118753.explanation: We want people with disabilities or handicaps to fully participate in our society. Among other things, we want to promote the physical accessibility of buildings, homes, public roads, and events by all possible means.

118754.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage, and an extra home on the premises can be a good solution. We stimulate the construction of (pre-)caregiver homes. This should remain limited to one or two houses.

118756.explanation: It is good that the municipality financially supports the local broadcaster, but this does not involve extra money.

118758.explanation: As a child, you are under pressure from addictive social media and performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. Where necessary, the municipality steps in. This offers every child equal opportunities.

118764.explanation: Everyone has the right to a safe home. Domestic violence, abuse, and neglect still often go unnoticed. We support the regional approach of Veilig Thuis and want the municipality to actively support this task and raise awareness.

118769.explanation: We especially want to build in existing neighborhoods and village centers. We preserve the green countryside as much as possible. New neighborhoods will be located, for example, on outdated industrial and greenhouse horticulture sites or on empty spaces within the municipal built-up area.

118770.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Adding extra requirements only creates more uncertainty. Motivating people to socially meaningful work is, of course, fine.

118772.explanation: We are launching a municipal poverty offensive: we are increasing income support. We pay extra attention to families with children and residents with multiple problems.

118775.explanation: Within built-up areas, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is allowed. The data clearly show: a lower speed limit makes traffic much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with limited mobility, this makes a world of difference.

118776.explanation: Everyone has the right to good care. Religion and beliefs must not play any role in this.

118777.explanation: In youth care, the priority is not the price but providing help that matches the concerns of our youth. We are critical of the current procurement practices. Carefully built relationships fall victim when care services are awarded to another provider.

118778.explanation: In recent years, the property tax (OZB) has hardly increased. By remaining critical of efficiency within the municipal apparatus, we try to keep this increase limited. A slight increase in local taxes may be necessary to achieve social goals.

118779.explanation: It seems more reasonable to tax only from a second dog onwards. But this is administratively very difficult. Therefore, we can accept the current scheme.

118782.explanation: The current Sunday shopping arrangement does not need to be changed. However, we also see disadvantages to Sunday openings and the 24-hour economy in general, such as increased workload, pressure on work-life balance, and competitive pressure between large chains and local independents.

118783.explanation: Maasdriel must contribute its fair share to the reception of refugees and status holders, together with other municipalities. Status holders are not the only ones who receive priority; priority may also be needed, for example, for residents with care needs and in cases of divorce.

118785.explanation: But only if it concerns AGRI PV. A new development where agricultural activity remains combined with solar panels. A field of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy.

118789.explanation: We aim for at least 40% social rental housing in new construction, also in the expensive neighborhoods. We will build more social rental homes by empowering housing corporations and setting clear requirements for project developers. This will make the search time for social rental housing acceptable again.

118790.explanation: Hard agreements must be made with housing corporations and developers about the type of new construction: smaller and primarily intended for starters and the elderly. High-rise buildings are not excluded, provided they fit the scale of the neighborhood. Five floors is a realistic limit.

118791.explanation: The rules for cohabitation, subletting, and splitting homes will be made more accessible and we will relax them. We want to lower the costs for the required permits. This way we make better use of the existing housing stock.

118792.explanation: Not only for safety but also to make it more pleasant for shoppers and recreational visitors, we choose a low-car center for Kerkdriel.

118793.explanation: The municipality must ensure that the household budget remains in order for many matters. Relocating the sand companies must not happen at any cost.

118794.explanation: Kerkdriel needs a meeting place in the village center so that everyone can easily get there.

118795.explanation: Maasdriel has agreed regionally to realize 5 wind turbines. Locally generated electricity will be needed in the future, especially with the arrival of local energy storage. Therefore, we are not waiting for the network capacity to be expanded.

118796.explanation: The municipality must make a careful consideration of how the available money for sports facilities is spent. For example, less can be spent on football fields (through club mergers), leaving more money for other sports facilities such as BMX cycling.

118797.explanation: That costs so much money that there isn't enough left for other important matters. It is more important to invest in good bicycle and public transport connections, so that there is less car traffic.

118739.explanation: D66 would like more participation from residents, for example via a referendum.

118744.explanation: D66 wants to further develop business parks with local parties to provide space for local entrepreneurs.

118746.explanation: D66 believes that the responsibilities of employer and housing provider should be separated as much as possible.

118750.explanation: In view of the fireworks ban, D66 wants to offer a fireworks show as an alternative. This can be organized every year in a different part of the municipality.

118751.explanation: At this moment, the residents of Maasdriel are doing well to bring this to the recycling center. People can usually leave it there for free.

118753.explanation: D66 wants that people with disabilities can participate fully in society. The municipality can help financially by, for example, granting permits free of charge.

118754.explanation: D66 wants solutions for the housing shortage. When a farm stops, houses can be built in its place. We want to build new homes as much as possible in the town centers.

118756.explanation: D66 considers the local broadcaster important for democracy and informing residents.

118758.explanation: Musical education is important for the development of young children. Income-dependent subsidies help make this possible.

118764.explanation: At this moment we can use the facilities in the region; if these facilities do not have enough places, we want to provide them in Maasdriel.

118769.explanation: D66 wants solutions for the housing shortage. We want to build new homes as much as possible in the town centers.

118770.explanation: D66 wants people to be able to participate in society. Meaningful social work sounds like a paid job and deserves an appropriate salary.

118772.explanation: The municipality must provide customized solutions where necessary to combat poverty.

118775.explanation: For safety reasons, D66 wants to reduce the maximum speed in some places. This does not apply to the entire built-up area.

118776.explanation: D66 believes that the quality of care is more important than religion, culture, or belief.

118777.explanation: Youth is the future; when youth care is needed, quality is more important than price.

118778.explanation: D66 would like lower taxes where possible. To pay all costs, the current property tax rate (ozb) is necessary.

118779.explanation: The costs of collecting the dog tax do not sufficiently outweigh the income and social benefits.

118782.explanation: D66 gladly leaves the choice to the retailer whether to open the shop on Sundays.

118783.explanation: It is important that refugees who have been granted the right to stay in the Netherlands receive suitable housing as soon as possible and can contribute to our society.

118785.explanation: D66 wants us to first use the roofs of homes and businesses for solar panels. If solar panels are placed on agricultural land, we pay attention to nature and the landscape.

118789.explanation: It is important that a suitable home is available to everyone in the municipality. For this, cheaper rental and owner-occupied homes are needed.

118790.explanation: D66 wants solutions for the housing shortage. We want to build new homes as much as possible in the town centers. These can also be apartment buildings.

118791.explanation: D66 wants to follow national regulations on this. It is not up to the municipality.

118792.explanation: At this moment, there is too little support for making the center of Kerkdriel car-free.

118793.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to stimulate the relocation of sand companies in every possible way. But not at any cost.

118794.explanation: De Kreek is poorly accessible for many residents. Alternatives within the center are more suitable as meeting points.

118795.explanation: D66 sets high requirements for any potential placement of new wind turbines. These requirements were not applied to the current wind turbines.

118796.explanation: The municipality must support sports clubs and contribute to sports facilities. The financial contribution must be proportional to the number of users within the municipality of Maasdriel.

118797.explanation: Accessibility of Kerkdriel is important. The Southern Access Route (ZOR) was too expensive. D66 would like to realize a cheaper alternative.

118739.explanation: The municipal council represents the voters and has received the mandate to represent them.

118744.explanation: The SGP gives priority to local companies that want to grow and provide employment.

118750.explanation: We do not consider this the task of the municipality, but we will support local initiatives! However, this will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis.

118754.explanation: We need to consider this on a case-by-case basis.

118772.explanation: This must keep pace with cost developments.

118777.explanation: The SGP believes it is important to also pay attention to people seeking youth care based on a particular belief system! And that this care can also be offered.

118782.explanation: The SGP advocates for one common weekly day of rest on all Sundays for our residents and staff.

118785.explanation: These should be placed on company roofs.

118739.explanation: Listening to the residents is very important to Heart for Maasdriel; a tool for this is a referendum. Because the opinion of the residents of Maasdriel matters to Heart for Maasdriel.

118744.explanation: Heart for Maasdriel encourages new business parks and the expansion of existing business parks for our local entrepreneurs and opposes large block-style distribution centers.

118746.explanation: Heart for Maasdriel wants a broader view on housing for seasonal workers than only on a private company site.

118750.explanation: Despite a fireworks ban, it is important to organize a fireworks show to strengthen the connection between residents.

118751.explanation: Free bulky waste collection lowers barriers, prevents illegal dumping, makes it fairer for residents without transport, and keeps Maasdriel cleaner and safer.

118753.explanation: Heart for Maasdriel supports people with disabilities, and resources must be made available for this.

118754.explanation: Farmyards can be a small-scale addition to the housing stock, especially when it concerns 1–3 homes on or adjacent to existing yards and buildings.

118756.explanation: The local broadcaster is already supported by both the municipality of Zaltbommel and the municipality of Maasdriel. There is no additional request for extra funds from the local broadcaster; therefore, the choice is none of the above.

118758.explanation: We believe every primary school child in Maasdriel should be able to take music lessons with support from the municipality. Music lessons contribute to equal opportunities: talent should not depend on parental income. Additionally, music strengthens children's broad development, well-being, and self-confidence.

118764.explanation: At the regional level, the organization of a women's shelter ('stay-away-from-my-man house') takes place.

118769.explanation: All opportunities must be utilized for additional housing for our residents, such as an extra street or a neighborhood at the village border.

118770.explanation: If it is feasible for the people.

118772.explanation: The municipality should combat poverty among residents rather than increase it.

118775.explanation: The municipality must comply with the legislation.

118776.explanation: Care indication is determined based on the law. However, we want people to be properly treated according to their wishes and needs.

118777.explanation: The quality is determined by law.

118778.explanation: Local taxes have risen enormously in recent years; taxes must remain affordable for residents.

118779.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair tax and is not directly spent on dogs.

118782.explanation: We want to enable shopping Sundays because this gives residents more choice and flexibility and helps our local shops compete with online offerings and neighboring municipalities. Shopping Sundays are not mandatory for entrepreneurs: each shop makes its own decision.

118783.explanation: The residents of Maasdriel should be given priority for social rental housing.

118785.explanation: Solar panels should be installed on roofs.

118789.explanation: Residents want an affordable social owner-occupied home in Maasdriel; we want to focus on this.

118790.explanation: All possibilities must be used to build new homes for our residents.

118791.explanation: All possibilities must be used to realize residential locations for our residents.

118792.explanation: In the past, we used to drive a lap around the square in Kerkdriel; that was a good time. Unfortunately, that is already no longer possible. However, further restrictions must be avoided.

118793.explanation: Buying out the sand companies is unaffordable for Maasdriel.

118794.explanation: The preference is for a central meeting place in the village of Kerkdriel. 'De Kreek' should be used for sports and event activities.

118795.explanation: No new wind turbines in Maasdriel.

118796.explanation: The Ammersoyen BMX club is a special association with a rich history and should be supported to stimulate sports.

118797.explanation: The ring road must be constructed on the existing road network in Kerkdriel and Velddriel.

statements.title: The municipality should contribute to a new track for Fietscrossclub Ammersoyen.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Maasdriel must do everything to ensure that the sand extraction companies leave the De Zandmeren recreation lake.

titleParts.text: Maasdriel moet koste wat kost zorgen dat de zandbedrijven verdwijnen bij

titleParts.glossary: A lake or pond where you are allowed to swim, windsurf and/or go boating.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
De Kreek should become the central meeting point of Kerkdriel.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Maasdriel should establish a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Maasdriel should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Maasdriel moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The residents of Maasdriel should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Maasdriel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Maasdriel should oppose new wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Maasdriel moet zich verzetten tegen nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The centre of Kerkdriel should be made car-free.
More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Maasdriel.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should construct a bypass around Kerkdriel.
